name: Tactacam Reveal X Pro
searchTerms: tactacam reveal x pro sd card
whySpecs: Tactacam specifies full-size SD only — microSD-to-SD adapters are explicitly discouraged and can cause read errors/ejection failures in the field.

faq.q: Can I use a microSD-to-SD adapter in the Reveal X Pro?
faq.a: No, Tactacam explicitly discourages microSD-to-SD adapters in this camera. Adapters have been linked to read errors and card ejection failures in the field, so use a genuine full-size SD card instead.

notes: Full-size SD only; no microSD slot on this model. Discourage adapters explicitly in copy.

name: Spypoint Flex

sdCard.maxCapacity: 32GB (larger cards are auto-reformatted down to 32GB by current firmware)

whySpecs: Officially supports up to 512GB, but current firmware caps/reformats any card over 32GB down to 32GB, so there's no benefit buying larger; formatting is via the camera's FORMAT button or a computer (FAT32), not mandatory through the app.

faq.q: Should I buy a 128GB or 256GB card for the Spypoint Flex?
faq.a: No, current firmware caps and auto-reformats any card over 32GB down to 32GB, so a larger card provides no extra usable storage. A 16GB or 32GB Class 10 microSD card is the practical choice.

notes: Source claimed in-app formatting was required — corrected; the app handles photo delivery/firmware, not formatting. Formatting is via the camera's FORMAT button or a computer (FAT32).

name: Bushnell Core DS-4K
whySpecs: 4K trigger video needs sustained write speed, Bushnell recommends U3/V30; one of Bushnell's first trail cams to break past the old 32GB ceiling.

faq.q: Can the Core DS-4K use cards larger than 32GB?
faq.a: Yes, unlike many older Bushnell trail cameras, the Core DS-4K supports up to 512GB, and its 4K trigger video benefits from the extra capacity — just make sure the card is rated U3/V30 or faster for sustained write speed.

notes: One of Bushnell's first trail cams past the old 32GB ceiling; emphasize U3/V30 for 4K trigger video.
